pattern: Identical mod-24 frequency-map approach as the Easy variant — the only difference is n up to 5×10^5, reinforcing that the O(n) solution is needed rather than O(n^2).
hints: Reduce each element to its remainder mod 24. Two remainders r1 and r2 form a complete day when r1 + r2 is 0 or 24.
Build a frequency array of size 24 and count complementary pairs. Special cases: remainder 0 pairs with remainder 0, remainder 12 pairs with remainder 12.
The answer is an int64 — with n up to 5×10^5, the count can reach ~n^2/2 ≈ 1.25×10^11, so use a long integer accumulator in JavaScript (Number is fine up to 2^53).

edge_inputs.description: very large hour values reducing to complementary remainders

stress.description: large random array for O(n) solution check
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, size)
    hours = [rng.randint(1, 10**9) for _ in range(n)]
    return [hours]
